title: 10 best herbal teas for hay fever
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hay fever are infusions made from dried flowers, leaves, seeds, or roots of various plants, which are believed to alleviate symptoms of hay fever. These teas are caffeine-free and can help soothe a sore throat, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Some popular herbal teas for hay fever include chamomile, nettle, and fenugreek. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce sinus congestion and alleviate itchy eyes. Nettle is known for its antihistamine effects, which can help reduce the production of histamines that cause allergic reactions. Fenugreek tea can help break down mucus and clear nasal congestion. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with hay fever can experience relief from their symptoms, allowing them to enjoy their daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with hay fever because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the yarrow plant, which are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses. Additionally, yarrow tea can boost the immune system and reduce allergic reactions, making it an effective natural remedy for hay fever. Its soothing and calming effects can also help reduce stress and anxiety associated with hay fever symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antihistamine: Yarrow tea contains antihistamine compounds that help alleviate hay fever symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and watery eyes by blocking the action of histamines.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of yarrow tea help protect the body from oxidative stress and boost the immune system, reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties help reduce mucus production and soothe irritated membranes, providing relief from hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of tannins.
Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and reduce discomfort associated with hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of volatile oils such as camphor, limonene, and azulene.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 tablespoon of dried yarrow material.
Rinse the harvested yarrow to remove any dirt or insects, then chop it finely.
Place the chopped yarrow in a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and put it in a cup.
Pour boiling water over the yarrow in the cup, and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or cheesecloth, and enjoy the warm yarrow tea for hay fever relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, yarrow flowers help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and volatile oils, yarrow leaves can reduce histamine release and allergic reactions.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds may help balance the immune response and provide relief from hay fever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tea helps with hay fever because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate symptoms such as a runny nose, itchy eyes, and congestion. The allicin in garlic is known to have decongestant properties that can help clear blocked nasal passages. Additionally, garlic has been found to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off allergens that trigger hay fever. Furthermore, garlic tea can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Drinking garlic tea regularly during hay fever season can provide relief and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garlic tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms. This is due to the presence of allicin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation.

Antihistamine: Garlic tea contains compounds, such as quercetin, that act as natural antihistamines, helping to alleviate hay fever symptoms by blocking the action of histamines, chemicals that the immune system produces in response to allergens.

Antioxidant: Garlic tea's high antioxidant content helps strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off allergens and reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms. Garlic contains various antioxidants, including allicin, vitamin C, and selenium.

Antimicrobial: Garlic tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections that can occur as a result of hay fever, such as sinus infections. The antimicrobial properties of garlic are attributed to allicin and other compounds like ajoene.
Decongestant: Garlic tea can help clear blocked nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. This is due to the heat generated by garlic, which can help thin mucus and reduce congestion.
remedy_recipe: Chop 3 cloves of garlic and let it sit for 10 minutes to activate its medicinal properties.
In a pot, combine 2 cups of water, chopped garlic, and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the garlic and chamomile.
Add honey and lemon to taste, then drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: The bulbs of garlic are commonly used to make medicinal teas for hay fever due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: Garlic leaves are used in teas to alleviate hay fever symptoms, as they contain natural antihistamines and have been shown to reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Garlic flowers are a less common but effective ingredient in teas for hay fever, as they possess antioxidant and anti-allergic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with hay fever because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. Mugwort has been shown to inhibit the release of histamines, which are chemicals that the immune system produces in response to allergens. By reducing histamine levels, mugwort tea can help to relieve inflammation and irritation in the nasal passages and respiratory tract. Additionally, mugwort has natural antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infections and promote overall respiratory health. Drinking mugwort tea regularly may therefore provide a natural and effective way to manage hay fever symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort tea helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms, due to its content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids.

Antihistamine: Mugwort tea contains antihistamine properties that help to reduce the release of histamines, which are responsible for allergic reactions in hay fever, thanks to its chamazulene and alpha-pinene constituents.

Antioxidant: Mugwort tea has potent antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress and boost the immune system, which is important for managing hay fever, as it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Immune modulator: Mugwort tea can help regulate and balance the immune system, reducing hypersensitivity to allergens, which is a key factor in hay fever, due to its polysaccharides and volatile oils.
Analgesic: Mugwort tea provides pain-relieving effects, which can help alleviate headaches and other discomforts associated with hay fever, due to its camphor and isothujone constituents.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the herbs.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mugwort herb (also known as Artemisia vulgaris) to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the mugwort herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a mug.
Enjoy your homemade mugwort tea, preferably without added sweeteners or milk, as they might interfere with the herb's medicinal properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hay fever.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids that have been shown to reduce histamine production, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with anti-allergic properties, making them useful for managing hay fever symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tea helps with hay fever because it contains natural antihistamine properties that can alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The tea is made from the bark, leaves, and twigs of the silver birch tree, which are rich in flavonoids and tannins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and pressure. Additionally, silver birch tea is a natural diuretic, which can help eliminate toxins from the body and boost the immune system. Drinking silver birch tea regularly during hay fever season can help manage symptoms and improve overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Silver birch tea contains betulinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms.
Antioxidant: The high flavonoid content in silver birch tea, such as quercetin, helps protect the body from oxidative stress and strengthen the immune system, reducing hay fever reactions.

Natural antihistamine: Silver birch tea includes methylated flavonoids, which can act as natural antihistamines, helping to relieve common hay fever symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes.
Diuretic: Silver birch tea promotes diuresis due to its saponin content, which aids in flushing out toxins and reducing fluid retention, helping to alleviate sinus pressure associated with hay fever.
Immune system support: The polyphenols and vitamins found in silver birch tea, such as vitamin C, support the immune system, reducing the body's susceptibility to allergens and hay fever triggers.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried silver birch leaves and 1 teaspoon of mixed herbs (such as nettle, chamomile, and elderflower) with 1 cup of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves and herbs.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, especially during hay fever season, to help alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, silver birch leaves are often used to make teas that help alleviate allergy symptoms, including hay fever.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, silver birch flowers can be used to make soothing teas that help reduce hay fever symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the silver birch tree is known for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular choice for making teas to treat hay fever.
Buds: Silver birch buds are packed with essential oils and antioxidants, making them a valuable addition to teas used to treat hay fever and other allergies.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with hay fever because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The flavonoids and carotenoids found in pot marigold have been shown to reduce histamine production, which is responsible for allergic reactions. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been used traditionally to soothe irritated membranes and promote respiratory health, making it a popular natural remedy for hay fever relief. Its calming effect on the nervous system can also help reduce stress and anxiety associated with seasonal allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids like lutein and zeaxanthin.
Antihistamine: The tea contains antihistamine properties that help to minimize the release of histamines, reducing allergic reactions, thanks to the active constituent quercetin.
Immune-modulating: Pot marigold tea supports a balanced immune response, which is crucial for managing hay fever symptoms, by influencing the activity of immune cells and reducing inflammation.
Antioxidant: The tea's high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and carotenoids, helps protect cells from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hay fever symptoms.
Diaphoretic: Pot marigold tea promotes sweating, helping the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever, which can help alleviate hay fever-related symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 grams of dried pot marigold flowers and rinse them under cold water to remove any impurities.
Boil 250 milliliters of water and add the rinsed pot marigold flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to be extracted from the flowers.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Pour the tea into a cup and enjoy, preferably drinking it 2-3 times a day for best results in managing hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are commonly used in making medicinal teas for hay fever due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nasal congestion and itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to help soothe respiratory symptoms associated with hay fever.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils and have been used in medicinal teas to help relieve symptoms of hay fever, including sneezing and runny noses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tea helps with hay fever because it contains cannabinoids, such as CBD, that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from symptoms like congestion, runny nose, and sneezing. Additionally, cannabis tea can also help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with hay fever, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. The herbal ingredients in the tea, such as chamomile and peppermint, can also have soothing and calming effects on the respiratory system, further helping to alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tea can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of cannabinoids such as CBD and THC.
Bronchodilator: Cannabis tea can help open up the airways and improve breathing, which is beneficial for hay fever sufferers experiencing congestion, thanks to the active constituent THC.
Antihistamine: Cannabis tea can help reduce the production of histamines, which are responsible for many hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of cannabinoids like CBD.
Immune system modulation: Cannabis tea can help regulate the immune system, reducing the overreaction that causes hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of various cannabinoids and terpenes.
Analgesic: Cannabis tea can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hay fever, such as headaches and sinus pressure, due to the presence of cannabinoids like CBD and THC.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1-2 grams of cannabis flower, preferably a strain high in CBD and low in THC, and place it in a tea infuser.
Heat 1 cup of water to boiling, then pour it over the infuser in a teapot or mug.
Allow the tea to steep for 15-20 minutes to extract the cannabinoids and herbal compounds.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice for flavor and additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
Discard the spent cannabis material and enjoy the tea up to three times a day for hay fever relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the cannabis plant contain cannabinoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hay fever.
Leaves: The leaves of the cannabis plant also contain cannabinoids and terpenes, and can be used to make medicinal teas to help reduce inflammation and irritation caused by hay fever.
Seeds: Cannabis seeds are rich in essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, making them a useful addition to medicinal teas for hay fever.

Stems: While not as potent as the flowers and leaves, the stems of the cannabis plant can still be used to make medicinal teas, providing a milder anti-inflammatory effect that can help relieve symptoms of hay fever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile tea helps with hay fever because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The tea's natural compounds, including chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and ease congestion. Additionally, chamomile tea has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for various ailments, including allergies, due to its calming and soothing effects on the body. Drinking roman chamomile tea regularly may help relieve hay fever symptoms and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman chamomile tea can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and irritation, due to its high content of anti-inflammatory compounds like azulene and apigenin.

Antihistamine: Chamomile tea contains flavonoids, such as apigenin and quercetin, that act as natural antihistamines, helping to relieve common hay fever symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and watery eyes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Roman chamomile tea, attributed to its polyphenol and flavonoid content, support overall immune function and help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hay fever symptoms.

Analgesic: Chamomile tea has analgesic properties, which can help alleviate headaches and other pain associated with hay fever, thanks to the presence of active constituents like matricin and bisabolol.

Sedative: The sedative effects of Roman chamomile tea, due to its content of apigenin and other volatile oils, can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the chamomile.
Pour the hot water into a cup and add 2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers (or 1 tea bag).
Cover the cup and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds from the chamomile.
Strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers and discard them.
Sip the tea slowly, several times a day, to relieve hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help alleviate hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves can help soothe irritated membranes and reduce histamine production, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
Seeds: High in essential oils, chamomile seeds can help alleviate congestion and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating hay fever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with hay fever because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms like sneezing, congestion, and runny nose. Additionally, the tea has been shown to boost the immune system and reduce the production of histamines, which are chemicals that the body releases in response to allergens. By drinking eastern purple coneflower tea, individuals may experience a reduction in the severity and frequency of hay fever symptoms, allowing them to enjoy the outdoors without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains echinacea, which helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms.
Immune-boosting: The tea's active constituents, such as alkamides and cichoric acid, support the immune system, helping the body fight off hay fever symptoms.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hay fever.
Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent secondary infections that may occur as a result of a weakened immune system due to hay fever.
Anti-allergenic: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains compounds that can help reduce histamine production and allergic responses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or cover the teapot to prevent further steeping.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, to help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of eastern purple coneflower have anti-allergic properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, coneflower leaves can reduce inflammation and provide relief from hay fever-related congestion and sneezing.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and fatty acids, coneflower seeds can help soothe irritated nasal passages and support respiratory health during hay fever episodes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with hay fever because it contains an anti-inflammatory compound called silicon, which can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. Additionally, horsetail tea is known to have antioxidant properties that can help boost the immune system and reduce the body's allergic response to pollen. Furthermore, horsetail tea can help soothe irritated membranes in the throat and nose, providing relief from the discomfort of hay fever. Drinking horsetail tea regularly during hay fever season can help manage symptoms and improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms.
Antihistamine: Horsetail tea contains compounds with natural antihistamine properties, like silica and calcium, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce hay fever symptoms.
Astringent: The astringent properties of horsetail tea, due to its tannin content, can help tighten and soothe mucous membranes, providing relief from hay fever-induced irritation and inflammation.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties, primarily due to its high potassium and silica content, which can help reduce fluid retention and alleviate sinus pressure associated with hay fever.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help protect the body from oxidative stress and support the immune system, which can help manage hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Place the horsetail in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the horsetail.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain out the herbs.
Drink the tea up to three times a day for hay fever relief.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and antioxidants, making them helpful in reducing inflammation and histamine production, which can alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-allergic properties, providing relief from hay fever symptoms such as sneezing, itching, and watery eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with hay fever because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and help alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, congestion, and a runny nose. Fennel is also known for its antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce the production of histamines in the body that are responsible for allergic reactions. Additionally, fennel contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bronchial smooth muscles, making it easier to breathe. Drinking fennel tea can also help to soothe a sore throat and ease coughing, common symptoms associated with hay fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating hay fever symptoms, due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, limonene, and anisic aldehyde.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of fennel tea, attributed to flavonoids like rutin and quercetin, help protect the body from oxidative stress and support overall immune function, which can be beneficial during hay fever season.
Antihistamine: Fennel tea has natural antihistamine properties, which help to minimize the release of histamines in the body, reducing hay fever symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes.

Bronchodilator: The bronchodilator properties of fennel tea, thanks to the presence of volatile oils, help to open up the airways and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe during hay fever attacks.
Calming: Fennel tea has calming and soothing properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with hay fever symptoms, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Chop one tablespoon of dried fennel seeds and let them steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the fennel tea to remove the seeds.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
Discard any leftover tea after 24 hours.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in teas for hay fever due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as nasal congestion and itchy eyes.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain antioxidants and have been used in teas to provide relief from hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation and histamine production.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have been used in medicinal teas for hay fever due to their ability to soothe irritated respiratory tracts and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Fennel stems can be used in teas to help alleviate hay fever symptoms such as sneezing and runny noses, as they contain compounds that act as natural antihistamines.
Rhizomes: Fennel rhizomes are used in teas for hay fever due to their bronchodilatory effects, which can help improve breathing and reduce inflammation in the airways.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hay fever with herbal teas is by using a variety of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant-rich herbs that soothe and heal the respiratory system. Look for teas made from stinging nettle, butterbur, ginger, licorice root, or turmeric. These herbs are known to reduce inflammation, support the immune system, and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, congestion, and itchy eyes. Drink at least two to three cups daily for optimal relief. However, always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hay fever that are treated with herbal teas include allergic rhinitis and sinusitis. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Meanwhile, ginger tea has natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, nettle tea is often recommended for allergies as it contains antihistamine compounds that can help control symptoms of hay fever. Licorice root tea is another herbal remedy that can soothe inflamed membranes and provide relief from coughing and congestion. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any herbal remedy, especially for those with underlying medical conditions or those taking prescription medications.
